verdict_reasoning: TGOLN2/TGN46 is a type I integral membrane protein with a transmembrane domain and luminal N-terminal extracellular domain. While predominantly localized to the trans-Golgi network (TGN), extensive literature documents constitutive cycling between the TGN and cell surface via endocytic pathways. The protein reaches the PM via its own mechanism (type I TM architecture with endocytic recycling signals), not via reversible non-covalent recruitment. This represents a stable dual-localization phenotype with a minority surface pool.
